description:
Spec landed in docs/architecture.md §2/§9/§ id-generation (bl-d7a5, commit 7ec94d4). Implement changes #2/#3 of that fold (shared recency-reconstruction machinery):

#3 `bl show <id>` (src/reads/show.rs): on a LIVE miss (tasks/<id>.md absent), walk balls/tasks history newest->oldest and reconstruct from the most recent commit whose tree still holds tasks/<id>.md, STOP at first hit. Render the dead ball with retirement derived from the deletion commit's bl-op: trailer (§5, close vs drop).

#2 `bl list` (src/reads/list.rs): keep defaulting to the live/open set; add --closed (dead only) / --all (live+dead), reconstructing dead balls from history the same most-recent-down way; add date/range, --tag, and text filters that COMPOSE (AND) over the possibly-history-served set. Date filters read stored created/updated (deletion-commit date for dead balls).

UNIFYING DISCIPLINE — recency resolution (§ id generation): every id->content lookup is one walk (live first, else most-recent-in-history, stop at first hit). This is the single helper #2 and #3 share; factor it once. Collision stays checked against LIVE files only (no prevent-reuse history scan — already spec'd). Unblocks bl-934a's discipline assumption. Independent of bl-6d9b (the ready->--status verb subtraction).

issue.last_synced_title: §9 impl: git-log-as-content reads — `show` history fallthrough + `list --closed/--all` + filters [bl-206c]

title: §9 impl: git-log-as-content reads — `show` history fallthrough + `list --closed/--all` + filters
